codex_response:
Each nonzero object is a monochrome copy of the same glyph; the output moves every object vertically so its top row matches the top row of the color-8 object, while keeping color, shape, and column position unchanged. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint because it matched the official examples; `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` had no entry for `e41c6fd3`, so no `notes.md` was needed. The generator stays on-family by sampling from the official-style glyph templates, keeping all copies identical within an instance, using 3-5 unique colors including `8`, and varying only vertical offsets and compact horizontal spacing. No new primitive was needed.

Verification passed on `4/4` official examples, the shared builder produced `1000` examples, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task e41c6fd3` passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked a generated preview.
